groupSummaries.summary: Exhaustively tested Group A (projects/folders, tables, columns, rows incl. server-side filter/sort/paginate, CSV import/export, stats, jobs/status, docs, and a per-endpoint security pass) against LIVE prod https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app inside a self-created sandbox workbook (now fully torn down, zero residue). Happy paths overwhelmingly PASS with re-fetch verification: project/folder CRUD + depth-limit(5) + 409 folderNotEmpty + 404-on-deleted, table/column/row CRUD, cell-merge on single+bulk PATCH, X-Total-Count increment/decrement, all 11 filter operators, AND/OR logic, sort asc/desc, limit/offset/past-end. The known P0 column-scope trap is CLOSED on BOTH filter and sort (foreign columnId -> 400 invalidColumnIds echoing the bad id + tableId). Security is solid: protected endpoints 401 unauth, bad token -> 401 'Invalid API key', public routes (/api/auth, /api/docs/download) 200 unauth. Found 9 real bugs. HEADLINE P1s: (1) CSV-imported rows persist a corrupted createdAt (year 58404) and empty updatedAt while POST /api/rows rows are correct -- isolated to the import insert path, confirmed in raw JSON; (2) the row WRITE path (POST and PATCH /api/rows) accepts foreign-sheet columnIds with NO validation -- asymmetric to GET which rejects them -- silently persisting orphaned, UI-invisible cells; (3) DELETE /api/rows with no tableId still deletes by id alone (missing scoping on a destructive op). Plus P2s: invalid project/column type accepted (201, no enum validation), bad projectId/tableId -> 500 instead of 400/404, column reorder creates duplicate order values, DELETE of a nonexistent id reports deletedCount:1, intermittent ~25s GET /api/rows timeouts through the Vercel->Azure proxy. CSV type-inference bug #9 CONFIRMED (empty first-row cell mistypes column as text; control proves inference works when row0 populated). Export bug #6 NOT reproduced -- formulas export their computed value, not the literal {{...}}, and enrichment hidden metadata does not leak. Zero paid external calls made.

Tested AI enrichment (setup-and-run, run, retry-cell, extract-datapoint, CRUD, jobs, batch, web-search) and formulas (run/rerun/generate + CRUD) against the live prod deployment (https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app) inside an isolated QA workbook (8f43da79-230e-4e00-b3f1-6c6ef27624b7, now deleted). Core happy paths PASS and persist correctly: setup-and-run created a single enrichment column (no Data Guide auto-columns), populated enrichmentData (city=Redmond/country=United States) with metadata.totalCost>0; run-on-existing-config, retry-cell, and extract-datapoint (materialized a text column) all verified by re-fetch; formula run/rerun produced correct persisted values; formula CRUD fully works; validation/auth (400/404/401) is correct; negative web-search control returned webSearchCalls=0; batch+webSearchEnabled correctly 400s. THREE serious bugs confirmed: (1) cost-cap-dead [P0] — costLimitEnabled+maxCostPerRow=0.00001 did NOT prevent a $0.0004122 run (41x over cap); (3) formula-timeout [P1] — an infinite-loop formula hung with no server-side eval timeout, pinning a worker; and as a direct consequence, intermittent worker-pool starvation degraded the whole backend (same GET endpoint alternately returned in <1.5s or hung >25s). Batch enrichment is effectively unusable through this deployment: batch submit and batch/status both 502/hang at ~120s (Vercel router ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR), and web-search positive path 502'd at 120s. Also found enrichment PATCH silently ignores the name field (and 400s on name-only PATCH), and formula/run on a bad tableId returns 500 instead of 4xx.

Tested find-email (Ninjer/TryKitt/AI-Ark + webhook + retry-cell) and lookup (run + retry-cell) against the live prod deployment (https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app) inside my own sandbox workbook QA__C_email_lookup__undefined (id d1c8549c-e916-47b1-b3b1-043431c0bd58) with a People sheet (4 real + 1 bogus + 1 empty-domain) and a Companies sheet (incl. two dupe.com rows with different Industry for the dup-key trap). Headline results: (1) Lookup happy path works and persists ALL source fields into enrichmentData; (2) CONFIRMED dup-key last-write-wins collision (bug #5) — domain dupe.com silently returns only Dupe Beta/IndustryBeta, Dupe Alpha is dropped; (3) MISSING foreign-column validation on BOTH lookup/run and find-email/run — a foreign columnId is accepted (200) instead of 400 invalidColumnIds, and on find-email it writes an orphan error cell into the target row under a foreign column id (silent cross-sheet pollution, P1); (4) lookup with an unknown sourceTableId returns 200 sourceRowCount:0 instead of erroring, and a foreign-input-column lookup run silently WIPED previously-matched cells; (5) AI-Ark async webhook round-trip fully works (Marc -> mbenioff@salesforce.com VALID); (6) TryKitt works (not_found, no sentinel leak in cell.value); (7) Ninjer is provider-misconfigured upstream (404 'Application not found' on every row) -> recorded SKIPPED_NO_PROVIDER; (8) email-sentinel-leakage bug #7 could NOT be reproduced on the TryKitt non-waterfall not_found path — cell.value stayed null (PASS); retry-cell works for both lookup and find-email; validation 400s and 401 unauth behave correctly. Paid provider attempts kept tiny (~6 billable, est <$0.05).

Tested concurrency/race conditions against live prod (https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app) inside my own sandbox workbook 112c4f93-7c0b-48b2-a584-4db589abf897 (Seed sheet, 2 rows). HEADLINE: (1) Enrichment double-run is non-atomic — two simultaneous POST /api/enrichment/run for the SAME config+targetColumn+row BOTH ran the model and charged distinct costs (A=$0.0000349/75 out-tok, B=$0.0000861/203 out-tok); the final cell is coherent (last-writer-wins, value=OK, no metadata corruption) but it is a real double-spend with no job-claim guard (P0 uncapped duplicate spend). (2) Campaign launch is NOT idempotent — two identical concurrent POST /api/campaigns produced two independent campaigns AND two separate top-level workbooks (69b446f0…, b68e5425…), each fully built with a Probe sheet + 2 rows, both reaching status=complete (P1; would be P0 double-spend on a plan with paid search/AI/email steps). (3) AI Ark email-finder is configured and exhibits the same double-submit race — two concurrent /api/find-email/ai-ark submits for one row+resultcol both returned submitted (4 AI Ark API calls for one logical cell); the cell still resolved coherently to status=complete / not_found(done) with a single provider record. (4) Bonus: discovered a missing-validation/data-integrity bug — POST /api/enrichment/run accepts a targetColumnId that is NOT a column of the table (e.g. 00000000-…), runs+charges the LLM, and writes an orphan column key into the row's data JSON (confirmed by re-fetch; the id is not in GET /api/columns). Negative controls passed: unauth→401, bad configId→404, empty steps→400, missing name→400, concurrent cell PATCH is coherent last-writer-wins with siblings intact. Paid usage tiny (gpt-5-nano + AI Ark, ~10 paid units, est ~$0.0004 model spend + minor AI Ark credits).

Performance/scale testing of DataFlow prod (https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app) using pure CRUD/query load (no paid calls). Created sandbox workbook 5dd8d0e0-e60d-4549-8485-3c01bc327b68, bulk-seeded one sheet to 1,000 -> 5,000 -> 10,000 rows (each row 5 text cells) via 500-row chunked POST /api/rows. WRITE path scales linearly and cleanly: ~137-141 rows/sec at every size (each 500-row chunk ~3.4-3.7s, no degradation 1k->10k). READ path confirms the documented in-JS load-whole-table architecture (bug #8): GET /api/rows latency tracks TOTAL table size, not result size. Smoking gun: at N=10,000 a limit=1&offset=0 request returning a single 446-byte row still takes ~894ms, and a contains filter returning 504KB takes 2.8s while scanning all 10k rows (X-Total=10000, X-Filtered=1111). Full no-filter fetch climbs 2.45s(1k)->3.47s(5k)->5.5s(10k) for a 4.5MB body; sortBy similar. Pagination boundary bugs found: negative limit=-1 returns N-1 rows (JS slice(0,-1) leak) and negative offset=-5 silently returns 0 rows. Folder depth guard fires correctly at depth 6 (5 nesting levels allowed) but ALL 400 errors (depth guard, folder-under-workbook) return EMPTY bodies. THE HEADLINE FAILURE: POST /api/formula/run over 10,000 rows is synchronous/inline with no row cap and no async job — it blew past the Vercel->ACA proxy's 120s limit (ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR), wrote ZERO data, created NO output column, AND rendered the ENTIRE backend (all endpoints, all sheets) unavailable for several minutes; write-path recovery lagged read-path by ~25s+. Formula correctness itself verified PASS on a 3-row sheet (8000->Enterprise, 2000->Mid, 100->SMB). Reached sizes: 10,000 rows. All sandbox resources (workbook + 5-deep folder chain) torn down and verified gone.

Drove a full realistic pipeline (CSV import -> server-side filter -> cross-sheet lookup -> 1 paid AI enrichment on gpt-5-nano -> CSV export -> re-import round-trip) inside my own workbook QA__C4_data_integrity_e2e__run (00a4532a-714b-4e3c-99ff-ce8d7769a228, now deleted), asserting persisted state by re-GET after EVERY step. Core data movement is functionally correct: CSV import infers the right typed columns (email/url/number/date/text) and lands all 5 rows; server-side filters return correct X-Filtered-Count incl. numeric greater_than/between on string-stored numbers; lookup carries the FULL source row into enrichmentData per-row with a __no_match sentinel for misses; the paid AI enrichment ($0.00025) persists structured enrichmentData + metadata and does NOT auto-create leak columns; PATCH /api/rows/{id} updates one cell and merges (other columns preserved); plain-column round-trip is byte-faithful (0 value mismatches, types preserved). Headline BUG (export fidelity / bug #6): the CSV export does NOT leak {{...}} formula literals (formula column exports computed values), but enrichment-typed columns export internal PLACEHOLDER strings -- lookup cells export the literal 'matched' and JSON-mode AI cells export '5 datapoints' instead of the actual enriched values; the real enrichmentData (San Francisco, Software, etc.) never reaches the CSV, so it is permanently lost on export and on export->reimport round-trip. Secondary bugs: POST /api/export/csv with an unknown tableId HANGS/times out (>25s) instead of 400/404; and the Vercel->Azure front-door intermittently returns ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR (5xx) on otherwise-valid reads -- a burst probe saw 7/10 GET /api/columns and 4/6 GET /api/stats fail while GET /api/projects passed 6/6 in the same window (transient, recovers on retry, no data corruption). Minor: GET /api/rows with an unknown-but-well-formed tableId returns 200 [] rather than 404. 1 paid call total.

Tested the campaigns engine, Agent X conversations, the campaign cron, and all destructive cascade/delete paths against live prod (https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app). Created my own sandbox workbook QA__D_campaigns_agent_destructive__undefined plus 6 campaign-spawned top-level workbooks; all torn down at the end (verified 404). Core happy paths, lifecycle transitions (pending->running->complete), cancel/delete, retry semantics, cascade-delete (no orphaned configs/jobs), nuke-table, and folder-not-empty (409) all PASS with re-fetch/data-movement verification. Two real defects found: (P1) the cron has NO concurrency lock — 3 parallel /api/cron/process-campaigns ticks all claimed to process the SAME step (import_csv ran "complete" twice in one race), which is safe only because import happened to be idempotent (row count stayed 3, not 6) but would double-bill paid steps (search/find_emails); and (P2) POST /api/campaigns accepts an unknown step type at creation (no schema validation) and only fails at runtime after wastefully auto-creating a workbook. Notable lesser issues: the stale-column-map case is handled gracefully (no crash) but deleting a column leaves orphaned cell data inside row.data keyed by the dead column id; nearly every 4xx/409 error response has an EMPTY body (no error code/message), hurting DX; cron stepping is inconsistent (sometimes 1 step/tick, sometimes multiple, plus an eager synchronous advance that makes a tick report "No active campaigns" while a step it just finished shows complete). Paid usage: 1 gpt-5-mini conversation turn + 1 AI-Ark preview (estimatedTotal 0, ~$0) + 1 gpt-5-nano setup-and-run ($0.00009). Approval "approve" turn correctly short-circuited the LLM (no charge).

teardown.summary: Teardown complete; zero sandbox objects remain. NOTE: the orchestrator's template substitution failed, so runId, base URL, and the sandbox folder id all arrived as the literal string "undefined". Recovered real values: base URL https://dataflow-pi.vercel.app (from scripts/test_result_columns.ps1) and the actual sandbox root folder QA-SANDBOX__qa_7a222842__20260608T081843Z (id 0d732428-9801-46d0-ad68-f5aad7ced3e9), which was the SOLE remaining project and the unambiguous QA sandbox root for this run (today's timestamp 20260608, QA-SANDBOX__ prefix, empty). Step 1 (explicit workbook ids): 4 of 18 were live and deleted (d1c8549c, 112c4f93, 69b446f0, b68e5425); the other 14 returned HTTP 404 (already gone in prior runs) and were ignored per idempotency. Step 2: GET /api/projects returned only the QA-SANDBOX folder; its nested children[] and tables[] were both empty (all child workbooks already removed), and NO project name contained the literal substring "undefined". Deleted the empty sandbox root folder via DELETE /api/projects/0d732428-... (succeeded with no 409 because it was already empty). Step 3: re-GET /api/projects returned 0 projects; an explicit filter for names matching /undefined/ OR QA-SANDBOX* found nothing. No objects could not be deleted, so leaked[] is empty. No non-matching objects were touched.

findings.scenario: Create workbook + nested folders under sandbox; PATCH rename; re-fetch to confirm persistence
findings.evidence: POST workbook 201 id=f1e9cf60; nested folder2 parentId set correctly; PATCH name -> 200, GET re-fetch returned 'QA__A_auth_crud_security__run_RENAMED'
findings.expected: 201 create, 200 rename, name persists
findings.actual: All as expected

findings.scenario: Folder depth limit (nest folders until rejected)
findings.evidence: 6th nesting level -> 400 {"error":"maxDepthExceeded","maxDepth":5}
findings.expected: Depth capped
findings.actual: Capped at 5 levels

findings.scenario: DELETE empty folder (ok) vs folder containing a workbook (expect 409); GET deleted project (expect 404)
findings.evidence: empty folder DELETE -> 200 {success:true}; GET deleted -> 404 {error:Project not found}; non-empty folder DELETE -> 409 {error:folderNotEmpty,childCount:1}; folder+child both still present after failed delete
findings.expected: 200 / 404 / 409 folderNotEmpty
findings.actual: Exactly as documented

findings.scenario: Create project with invalid type:'banana' (enum should be folder|workbook|table)
findings.evidence: Response 201 {"id":"71085fb2...","type":"banana"} — project created with bogus type
findings.expected: 400 invalid type (enum validation)
findings.actual: 201 Created with type='banana' persisted
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: POST /api/projects validates presence of name+type but not that type is in the allowed enum

findings.scenario: Create project with non-existent parentId
findings.evidence: 400 {"error":"parentNotFound"}
findings.expected: 400 parentNotFound
findings.actual: 400 parentNotFound (FK validated here, unlike tables/columns)

findings.scenario: Create/list/PATCH-rename/GET sheet (table) in workbook
findings.evidence: Sheet1/Sheet2 created 201; GET ?projectId list count=2; PATCH name -> 200 'Sheet2_RENAMED' verified via GET /api/tables/{id}
findings.expected: Full CRUD works + persists
findings.actual: As expected

findings.scenario: Create table with missing projectId vs non-existent projectId
findings.evidence: missing projectId -> 400 {error:projectId and name are required} (good); BUT non-existent projectId 00000000... -> 500 {error:Failed to create table}
findings.expected: 400/404 projectNotFound for bad FK (matching projects' parentNotFound behavior)
findings.actual: 500 Failed to create table — DB FK error leaked as 500
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: tables route does not pre-validate projectId existence; FK constraint violation surfaces as unhandled 500

findings.scenario: Create each column type (text/number/email/url/date); list ordering; PATCH rename+width+type; reorder; DELETE
findings.evidence: All 5 types created 201 (width=150, auto order 0..5); list ordered by order; PATCH Score -> name='Score2' width=240 persisted on re-GET; DELETE -> 200 {success:true}, verified gone, count restored
findings.expected: All column ops succeed + persist
findings.actual: As expected

findings.scenario: PATCH column order=0 on a non-first column (reorder)
findings.evidence: Set Domain order=0; subsequent GET shows TWO columns at order 0: 'Name:0 | Domain:0 | Score:1 ...' — siblings not shifted
findings.expected: Reorder reindexes siblings to keep a total order (no duplicate order values)
findings.actual: Duplicate order=0 on two columns; display order becomes ambiguous/nondeterministic on the tie
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: PATCH writes the new order value without re-sequencing other columns in the table

findings.scenario: Create column with missing tableId vs non-existent tableId vs invalid type
findings.evidence: missing tableId -> 400 (good); non-existent tableId -> 500 {error:Failed to create column}; type='banana' -> 201 with type='banana' persisted
findings.expected: 400/404 for bad tableId; 400 for invalid type enum
findings.actual: 500 on bad tableId (FK leak); 201 accepts bogus column type
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: No FK pre-check for tableId; no enum check for column type

findings.scenario: Bulk insert 5 rows; GET with X-Total-Count header
findings.evidence: POST returned array of 5 inserted rows (201); GET bodyCount=5, X-Total-Count=5, X-Filtered-Count=5
findings.expected: 5 rows inserted, header=5
findings.actual: As expected

findings.scenario: Single PATCH /api/rows/{id} updating ONLY one cell — assert other cells intact (merge)
findings.evidence: Patched Score 10->999; re-fetch: Name=Alice, Email=alice@acme.com, Domain=acme.com all unchanged, Score=999
findings.expected: Merge keeps untouched cells
findings.actual: Merge correct

findings.scenario: Bulk PATCH /api/rows {updates:[...]} — assert per-row cell-merge
findings.evidence: 200 {success:true,updatedCount:2}; Bob Score 30->333 (Name/Email intact), Carol Email->carol2@acme.com (Score=20 intact)
findings.expected: Both rows merged correctly
findings.actual: As expected

findings.scenario: Bulk DELETE /api/rows — assert X-Total-Count decremented
findings.evidence: Before X-Total=5; DELETE 2 ids -> 200 {success:true,deletedCount:2}; after X-Total=3, bodyCount=3
findings.expected: Count 5 -> 3
findings.actual: Decremented correctly

findings.scenario: DELETE /api/rows with NO tableId in body (only ids)
findings.evidence: Body {ids:[validRowId]} with no tableId -> 200 {success:true,deletedCount:1}; the row was actually deleted from its sheet
findings.expected: 400 tableId required (POST /api/rows DOES require it), or at least sheet-scoped delete
findings.actual: Deletes a row by id alone with no sheet scoping — a caller can delete any row id without specifying/owning the target sheet
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: DELETE handler keys off ids[] and ignores tableId; no check that ids belong to the stated/any authorized table

findings.scenario: DELETE /api/rows with a non-existent row id
findings.evidence: ids:[00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000], tableId set -> 200 {success:true,deletedCount:1}
findings.expected: deletedCount:0 (nothing matched)
findings.actual: Reports deletedCount:1 for a row that does not exist — count is fabricated/inaccurate
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: deletedCount echoes ids.length rather than the actual affected-row count from the delete

findings.scenario: POST /api/rows missing tableId; POST empty rows array (boundary)
findings.evidence: no tableId -> 400 {error:tableId is required}; empty rows [] -> 201 [] (graceful no-op)
findings.expected: 400 on missing tableId; graceful empty insert
findings.actual: As expected

findings.scenario: Server-side filter operators: is_empty, is_not_empty, contains, not_contains, equals, not_equals, starts_with, ends_with, greater_than, less_than, between
findings.evidence: All 11 produced exact expected row sets vs known seed data (e.g. is_empty(Email)->Dave X-Filtered=1; between(Score,30,60)->Dave,Eve; greater_than(Score,100)->Alice,Bob). Required filters as a JSON ARRAY; an object (non-array) yields 400/parse error
findings.expected: Each operator filters server-side correctly
findings.actual: All correct (after retrying through intermittent backend timeouts — see perf finding)

findings.scenario: filterLogic AND vs OR with two filters; sortBy asc/desc; limit/offset; offset past end
findings.evidence: AND(acme & Score>50)->[Alice]; OR->[Alice,Bob,Carol]; sort Score asc->Carol,Eve,Dave,Bob,Alice; desc reversed; limit2/offset0->Carol,Eve; offset2->Dave,Bob; offset99->[] status200 X-Total=5
findings.expected: Correct AND/OR, ordering, pagination, graceful past-end
findings.actual: All correct

findings.scenario: FRAGILE column-scope: filter Sheet2 using Sheet1's 'Domain' columnId -> expect 400 invalidColumnIds echoing bad id (pre-fix silently matched all rows = P0 trap)
findings.evidence: 400 {error:'Filter references columnId(s) that do not belong to this table...',invalidColumnIds:[96701191...(Sheet1.Domain)],tableId:b0b5038c(Sheet2)}
findings.expected: 400 invalidColumnIds with the foreign id + target tableId
findings.actual: Exactly as expected — P0 silent-match trap is CLOSED

findings.scenario: FRAGILE column-scope: sortBy a foreign (Sheet1) columnId on Sheet2 -> expect 400 invalidColumnIds
findings.evidence: 400 {error:'sortBy references a columnId that does not belong to this table...',invalidColumnIds:[96701191...],tableId:b0b5038c}
findings.expected: 400 invalidColumnIds for sortBy too
findings.actual: Sort path also guarded correctly

findings.scenario: SECURITY: write a row to Sheet2 using a columnId that belongs to Sheet1 (POST /api/rows) -> expect rejection, not silent cross-write
findings.evidence: POST tableId=Sheet2 rows=[{Sheet1.Name id: {value:'CROSS-WRITE-ATTEMPT'}}] -> 201; row persisted in Sheet2 with data key ea006bf9...(Sheet1.Name). GET Sheet2 shows that row keyed by a column that does not exist in Sheet2
findings.expected: 400 invalidColumnIds (symmetric to GET filter/sort validation) — reject foreign cell keys
findings.actual: 201, silent orphaned-cell write. Cell is invisible in Sheet2 UI (no matching header), unreadable by Sheet2 filters/export — silent data corruption
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Write path (POST/PATCH rows) does not validate cell-data keys against the target table's columns, unlike the read/filter/sort path

findings.scenario: SECURITY: PATCH a row using a foreign (Sheet1) columnId
findings.evidence: PATCH Sheet2 row data={Sheet1.Score id: {value:12345}} -> 200; value stored under foreign key a4db8914... Sheet1 confirmed NOT polluted (isolation to target tableId holds, but foreign-key validation absent)
findings.expected: 400 invalidColumnIds
findings.actual: 200, foreign cell key accepted and persisted (same gap as POST)
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: PATCH row handler shares the unvalidated write path

findings.scenario: FRAGILE CSV type inference (bug #9): import CSV whose FIRST data row has EMPTY cells in columns that are numeric/email in later rows
findings.evidence: Rows [{Age:'',Contact:''},{Age:'42',Contact:'yan@corp.com'},{Age:'35',Contact:'xavier@corp.com'}] -> Age inferred type=text, Contact inferred type=text. CONTROL with populated row0 [{Age:'28',Contact:'anna@corp.com'},...] -> Age=number, Contact=email
findings.expected: Type inferred from non-empty values across the column (number/email)
findings.actual: Inference samples only row 0; empty leading cell mistypes the whole column as text (bug #9 confirmed)
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Type-inference reads data[0][col] only instead of scanning the first non-empty value

findings.scenario: FRAGILE export leak (bug #6): export a sheet containing a formula column and an enrichment column with data -> check formula exports value not literal {{...}} and whether enrichment/hidden columns leak
findings.evidence: CSV: header 'Person,Age,Label,AI Insight'; formula col 'Label' exported computed value 'Anna (28)' (NOT the {{...}} template); enrichment 'AI Insight' exported cell.value 'SECRET-ENRICH-VALUE' but the hidden enrichmentData blob {hidden:'leak-me'} did NOT appear. Clean Sheet1 export had exactly its 2 real rows
findings.expected: Formula -> evaluated value (bug #6 would show literal {{...}}); no hidden metadata leak
findings.actual: Bug #6 NOT reproduced — formula evaluated correctly; only cell.value of enrichment column exported (no enrichmentData leak)

findings.scenario: Data-movement: persisted createdAt/updatedAt on CSV-imported rows vs POST /api/rows rows (single-shot, no retry)
findings.evidence: Raw JSON: CSV-imported row createdAt='+058404-10-02T06:03:55.000Z' (YEAR 58404) and updatedAt empty; a POST /api/rows row in the SAME sheet createdAt='2026-06-08T08:53:12.537Z' (correct). Reproduced on 3 separate import sheets
findings.expected: createdAt = current date (2026-06-08)
findings.actual: CSV import writes a corrupted far-future createdAt (year 58404) and empty updatedAt; breaks any time-based sort/filter/audit on imported rows
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: import/csv likely stores Date.now() milliseconds into a column read as epoch-seconds (or new Date(ms) treated as new Date(seconds)) — ms->s scale error inflates the year

findings.scenario: CSV import mapping to an EXISTING column via targetColumnId; GET rows by specific rowIds
findings.evidence: import targetColumnId -> 200 {rowsImported:1,columnsCreated:0}; GET rowIds=bob,carol -> 200 count=2 (note: X-Total-Count/X-Filtered-Count headers were empty on the rowIds-filtered request)
findings.expected: Imports into existing column; returns the 2 requested rows
findings.actual: Correct; minor: count headers omitted when rowIds param is used

findings.evidence: both -> 400 {error:tableId is required}
findings.expected: 400 missing tableId
findings.actual: As expected

findings.expected: 200 with job/progress structures

findings.scenario: SECURITY: protected endpoints WITHOUT Authorization header -> expect 401; bad bearer token -> 401

findings.evidence: GET /api/rows no-auth 401; POST /api/columns no-auth 401; DELETE /api/projects no-auth 401; GET /api/projects bad-token 401 {error:'Invalid API key'}; no-auth message {error:'Authentication required...'}
findings.expected: 401 for all protected routes; distinct invalid-key message
findings.actual: As expected — auth enforced on read, write, and destructive routes

findings.evidence: GET /api/auth no-auth -> 200 {authenticated:false}; GET /api/docs/download no-auth -> 200, 60851-byte API reference markdown
findings.expected: Public routes reachable unauthenticated

findings.scenario: Reliability: latency/timeouts on core read endpoint under sequential load
findings.evidence: Multiple GET /api/rows calls intermittently hung to a hard 25s+ client timeout (status=0, 'operation has timed out') then succeeded on retry; same operator/filter returned in <1-2s on adjacent attempts. Non-deterministic (hit equals, then not_equals on different runs)
findings.expected: Consistent sub-2s reads
findings.actual: Sporadic ~25s+ stalls requiring retries; correctness unaffected but a reliability risk for an internal rollout
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Vercel->Azure Container Apps proxy cold starts or backend connection-pool exhaustion; intermittent, not tied to a specific operator

findings.scenario: FRAGILE formula eval timeout: infinite-loop formula on 1 row, 30s client timeout, ONE attempt

findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Formula evaluator runs untrusted JS synchronously (e.g. new Function / eval) on the request thread with no VM timeout/worker isolation; an infinite loop blocks the Node event loop indefinitely

findings.scenario: Worker-pool starvation downstream of unguarded eval / long-blocking batch calls
findings.expected: lightweight read endpoints return consistently in well under a second
findings.actual: Same endpoint, same input, non-deterministic: GET /api/columns returned 3x in <1.5s then hung >25s; GET /api/enrichment hung >25s 3x then returned in 1.5s; GET /api/rows fine one moment, hung the next. Began right after the infinite-loop formula + blocking batch submits.
findings.evidence: COLS try1-3 OK <1.5s, try4 FAIL 25s; ENRICH try1-3 FAIL 25s, try4 OK 1.49s
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Blocked workers (pinned by the infinite-loop eval and/or 120s-blocking Azure batch submits) starve the pool; requests routed to a stuck worker hang, others succeed — whole-deployment availability blast radius from the missing eval timeout

findings.scenario: Fire TWO concurrent POST /api/enrichment/run for the SAME config+targetColumn+row (1 row, gpt-5-nano). Assert final cell coherent AND whether both jobs ran/charged (double-spend).
findings.expected: A concurrent duplicate run for the same config+targetColumn+row should be claimed/de-duplicated so the model is invoked AT MOST once per cell (single charge), or the second request should no-op/short-circuit.
findings.actual: Both concurrent requests returned HTTP 200 success=true and EACH independently invoked the LLM and charged: Request A metadata={inputTokens:49,outputTokens:75,totalCost:0.0000349}; Request B metadata={inputTokens:49,outputTokens:203,totalCost:0.0000861}. Two distinct model calls billed for ONE logical cell. No claim guard. Final persisted cell is coherent (value=OK, status=complete, last-writer-wins = B's metadata; no partial-merge corruption) and column progress shows complete:1 — so integrity is OK but spend is doubled.
findings.evidence: A: {"cost":0.0000349, metadata outputTokens=75, time=1623ms, HTTP 200, time_total=2.60s}. B: {"cost":0.0000861, metadata outputTokens=203, time=2620ms, HTTP 200, time_total=3.62s}. Re-fetch final: cell.value=OK status=complete metadata.totalCost=0.0000861 (B won). GET /api/columns/{id}/progress cellStatuses.complete=1.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Run handler does SELECT row then a separate UPDATE with no row/cell-level lock or job-claim (no SELECT…FOR UPDATE / atomic compare-and-set on cell.status=processing), so two requests both pass the gate and both call the model — non-atomic claim → double-spend.

findings.scenario: POST /api/campaigns with the SAME tiny plan TWICE within a few hundred ms (concurrent background curls). Record whether two independent campaigns + two top-level workbooks are created (double-create).
findings.expected: Idempotent launch: two identical concurrent submissions should collapse to ONE campaign + ONE workbook (via an idempotency key/dedup), or the second should be rejected/return the first campaign id.
findings.actual: Both POSTs returned HTTP 201 with DIFFERENT ids and DIFFERENT workbooks: camp_d16cb809-f0b4-4751-82c4-ce7feb954385 (wb 69b446f0-9381-49da-8163-72ff9812a47e) and camp_12fe344f-26d9-430d-8bf9-55f14de2db1a (wb b68e5425-1276-475b-b0d7-dea993c2f366). Both ran to status=complete and each workbook independently built a 'Probe' sheet with 2 rows. Full double-create of campaign + workbook + data. For a plan containing paid search/AI/email steps this is a double-spend (P0-class).
findings.evidence: A: id=camp_d16cb809… workbookId=69b446f0… HTTP 201 status=running. B: id=camp_12fe344f… workbookId=b68e5425… HTTP 201 status=running. Poll: both FINAL status=complete. WB 69b446f0 → sheet 'Probe' rows=2; WB b68e5425 → sheet 'Probe' rows=2.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: No idempotency key or dedup on (name+steps hash) at campaign create; create_workbook step always inserts a fresh top-level workbook. Concurrent identical requests are treated as fully independent.

findings.scenario: Re-parent each duplicate campaign-created top-level workbook under the sandbox/QA workbook (PATCH /api/projects/{id} {parentId}) so teardown can find them.
findings.expected: Be able to re-parent the leaked campaign workbooks under the designated sandbox folder so teardown traversal finds them.
findings.actual: Sandbox folder id was provided as literal 'undefined' (no real folder id), so re-parenting under a sandbox folder was impossible. Attempting PATCH parentId=<my QA workbook id> returned HTTP 400 (workbooks cannot be nested under another workbook — only under folders). Both leaked workbook ids ARE recorded in workbookIdsCreated so teardown deletes them by id regardless of parent.
findings.evidence: PATCH /api/projects/69b446f0… {parentId: <wb>} => HTTP 400; PATCH /api/projects/b68e5425… {parentId: <wb>} => HTTP 400. Both ids captured into workbookIdsCreated for id-based teardown.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Sandbox folder id placeholder was unset ('undefined'); projects API correctly rejects workbook-under-workbook nesting. Not a product bug — environment/placeholder limitation. Recorded for teardown.

findings.scenario: Bonus: rapidly PATCH the SAME row cell from two concurrent requests with different values; assert last-writer-wins is coherent (no partial-merge corruption, siblings intact).
findings.expected: One writer wins cleanly; the other columns in the row are preserved; no half-merged/garbage cell.
findings.actual: Both PATCHes returned HTTP 200. Final re-fetch shows Note cell = WRITER_A (single clean winner) and the other two cells intact (Domain=shopify.com, Company=Shopify). No partial merge, no lost sibling cells. Correct last-writer-wins.
findings.evidence: PATCH A body Note=WRITER_A (200), PATCH B body Note=WRITER_B (200). GET /api/rows?rowIds=… => data['…Note…'].value=WRITER_A, Domain=shopify.com, Company=Shopify all present.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Row PATCH merge appears to be a single atomic JSON update per request; concurrent writes serialize to last-writer-wins without corrupting the merged object.

findings.scenario: Webhook double-fire (AI Ark): provider availability check + fire two concurrent POST /api/find-email/ai-ark submits for the SAME row+resultColumn (two trackIds → two webhooks racing into one cell). Assert final cell coherent.
findings.expected: AI Ark is configured (submit returns 'submitted'). A duplicate concurrent submit for the same cell should be de-duplicated so only ONE AI Ark search+email-finder pair is charged and only one webhook lands; final cell must be coherent.
findings.actual: Both concurrent submits returned HTTP 200 status=submitted, submittedCount=1 each — i.e. TWO independent AI Ark submissions (2 API calls each = 4 calls) for one logical cell, no claim guard (same double-spend class as enrichment/run). Final cell DID resolve coherently after webhooks: status=complete, value=null, enrichmentData.status='not_found (done)', single provider=ai_ark + single track_id. So integrity is OK (last-writer-wins on the cell) but AI Ark credits are double-charged. The HMAC token is identical for the same tableId:rowId:resultColId, so both webhook callbacks are valid and target the same coordinate.
findings.evidence: Single probe: {status:submitted, submittedCount:1, async:true}. Concurrent A & B: both {status:submitted, submittedCount:1, HTTP 200}. Poll after ~10s: cell={value:null,status:complete,enrichmentData:{email:null,status:'not_found (done)',track_id:'3fe407c6-…',person_id:'5f977a89-…',provider:'ai_ark'}}.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Same non-atomic claim issue as /enrichment/run — ai-ark submit path does not mark the target cell as claimed/processing before issuing AI Ark calls, so concurrent submits both proceed and both spend AI Ark credits; two webhooks with identical HMAC both write the same coordinate.

findings.scenario: Validation/data-integrity: POST /api/enrichment/run with a valid configId but a targetColumnId that is NOT a column of the table (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000). Should 400/404; assert it does not write/charge.
findings.expected: 400/404 invalidColumnId — targetColumnId must belong to tableId (mirrors GET /api/rows 400 invalidColumnIds on the read/filter path). No LLM call, no write.
findings.actual: Returned HTTP 200 success=true, ran the LLM and charged $0.0000349, and WROTE the result into a phantom cell keyed by 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 in the row's data JSON. Re-fetch confirms the orphan key persists in row.data and is NOT present in GET /api/columns?tableId=… (REAL_COL_IDS excludes it). The write path does not validate that targetColumnId is a real column of the table — pollutes row data with an orphan cell AND bills a model call.
findings.evidence: POST /api/enrichment/run {targetColumnId:'00000000-…'} => 200 {cost:0.0000349, data['00000000-…']={value:OK,status:complete,…}}. Re-fetch row data keys include '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' (ORPHAN_KEY_PRESENT=True); GET /api/columns => realIds do NOT contain it (ORPHAN_IS_REAL_COLUMN=False).
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: enrichment/run validates configId (404 on unknown) but not that targetColumnId is a column of tableId before merging the result into row.data — no membership check on the write path, unlike the read/filter path which now returns 400 invalidColumnIds.

findings.scenario: Negative control: POST /api/enrichment/run with an unknown configId.
findings.expected: 404 (or 400) for a non-existent config; no charge.
findings.actual: Returned HTTP 404 as expected.
findings.evidence: POST /api/enrichment/run {configId:'nonexistent_cfg_zzz'} => HTTP 404.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Config existence is validated before run.

findings.scenario: Negative control: POST /api/campaigns with empty steps[] and with missing name.
findings.expected: 400 for empty steps and for missing required name.
findings.actual: Both rejected: empty steps[] => HTTP 400; missing name => HTTP 400. (Note: docs say create_workbook is auto-prepended, but a fully empty steps array is still rejected, which is acceptable.)
findings.evidence: POST /api/campaigns {name:'…',steps:[]} => 400; POST /api/campaigns {steps:[create_workbook]} (no name) => 400.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Basic body validation present on campaign create.

findings.scenario: Auth control: call a core endpoint with no Authorization header.
findings.expected: 401 Unauthorized without a bearer token.
findings.actual: Returned HTTP 401 as expected.
findings.evidence: GET /api/projects with only Content-Type header => HTTP 401.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Bearer-auth middleware enforced on /api/* (except documented AI Ark webhook HMAC path).

findings.scenario: Bulk-seed 1,000 rows via chunked POST /api/rows (500/req) and measure insert throughput
findings.actual: 1000 rows inserted in 7082ms across two 500-row chunks (3586ms + 3429ms) = 141.2 rows/sec. POST returns the inserted row array (Count=500 per chunk).
findings.expected: Bulk insert succeeds and persists; throughput recorded.
findings.evidence: chunk [0..500)->3586ms, chunk [500..1000)->3429ms; SEEDED 1000 in 7082ms => 141.2 rows/sec. Re-fetch X-Total-Count=1000.

findings.scenario: Scale insert to 5,000 then 10,000 rows; check for write-path degradation
findings.actual: +4000 rows (to 5k) at 139.0 rows/sec; +5000 rows (to 10k) at 137.5 rows/sec. Per-500-chunk time stays flat ~3.4-3.7s from row 0 to row 10000 — insert path is O(batch), no degradation as table grows.
findings.expected: Insert throughput stays roughly constant as the table grows.
findings.evidence: batch2: 4000 rows in 28778ms=139/s; batch3: 5000 rows in 36364ms=137.5/s; final GET X-Total-Count=10000. Chunk times: 3371-4172ms throughout.

findings.scenario: GET /api/rows latency curve, no filter, at N=1000/5000/10000 (full-table fetch)
findings.actual: No-filter full fetch: 2450ms@1k (451KB) -> 3474ms@5k (2.27MB) -> ~5500ms@10k (4.54MB, two trials 5433/5624ms). Latency grows ~linearly with row count. Returns correct X-Total-Count each time.
findings.expected: Returns all rows with correct X-Total-Count; latency characterized.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Whole table is loaded into memory and serialized; cost scales with table size. Extrapolates to ~25-30s near 50k rows.

findings.scenario: GET /api/rows with contains filter at scale — verify filter runs in JS over the whole table (bug #8)
findings.actual: Contains filter on Company Name: 1388ms@1k -> 1814ms@5k -> 2827ms@10k. Latency scales with TOTAL rows scanned (X-Total), not the matched result size. At N=10000 it scans 10000 rows to return 1111 (504KB) in 2.8s. X-Filtered-Count is correct (111@1k, 111@5k, 1111@10k).
findings.expected: A DB-side WHERE/LIKE would make filtered-read latency depend on result size, not total table size. Filter cost should not grow with rows that don't match.
findings.evidence: N=10000 contains 'Company 7': 2827ms X-Total=10000 X-Filtered=1111 bytes=504366. N=1000 same filter: 1388ms X-Filtered=111. Filter result is correct; the cost model is the problem.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Backend loads entire table into memory then Array.filter() in JS (documented bug #8) — no DB-level predicate pushdown.

findings.scenario: GET /api/rows with limit=1 offset=0 at N=10000 — does pagination push the limit to the DB?
findings.actual: Fetching a SINGLE row (446-byte body) at N=10000 takes ~894ms; at N=5000 ~1143ms. A row-count-independent op (DB LIMIT 1) would be tens of ms. Latency tracks table size, proving the whole table is materialized before the JS slice.
findings.expected: limit=1 should be near-constant time regardless of table size (DB LIMIT/OFFSET).
findings.evidence: N=10000 'pagination limit=1 off=0' 894ms bytes=446 X-Total=10000; N=5000 1143ms bytes=446. Compare 'limit=50 off=9900' 1718ms@10k.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Pagination is applied via Array.slice() AFTER loading all rows into memory; the DB query has no LIMIT/OFFSET.

findings.scenario: GET /api/rows with sortBy at N=10000
findings.actual: sortBy Size desc: 2062ms@1k -> 3260ms@5k -> 4579ms@10k. Returns full table sorted (4.54MB@10k). Sort is in-JS over the in-memory table; latency comparable to no-filter fetch. No 5xx.
findings.expected: Sorted full-table read completes; latency characterized.
findings.evidence: N=10000 'sortBy Size desc' 4579ms X-Total=10000 bytes=4538621; N=5000 3260ms; N=1000 2062ms.

findings.scenario: Pagination boundary: negative limit (limit=-1) at N=10000
findings.actual: limit=-1 returns 9999 rows (N minus 1) instead of erroring or returning 0/all. This is raw Array.slice(0,-1) semantics leaking through — a client passing a bad/negative limit silently loses exactly the last row, a quiet data-truncation trap.
findings.expected: Negative limit should be rejected (400) or clamped, not interpreted as slice(0,-1) dropping the last row.
findings.evidence: 'negative limit (limit=-1)' -> 4884ms rows=9999 X-Total=10000 X-Filt=10000.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: limit is passed unvalidated into Array.prototype.slice(offset, offset+limit); negative limit makes the end index negative => drops trailing rows.

findings.scenario: Pagination boundary: negative offset (offset=-5) at N=10000
findings.actual: offset=-5 returns 0 rows (X-Total still 10000) instead of clamping to 0 and returning the first rows. A negative offset silently yields an empty page.
findings.expected: Negative offset should clamp to 0 (return first page) or 400, not silently return 0 rows.
findings.evidence: 'negative offset (off=-5)' -> 1049ms rows=0 X-Total=10000 X-Filt=10000.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: slice(-5, -5+10)=slice(-5,5) on a 10000-element array yields [] (start index after end); unvalidated negative offset.

findings.scenario: Pagination boundary: offset>=total, limit=0, huge limit at N=10000
findings.actual: offset=10001 -> 0 rows (correct); offset==10000 -> 0 rows (correct); limit=0 -> 0 rows (correct); limit=999999 -> all 10000 rows (correct). These edges behave sensibly; X-Total-Count stays 10000 throughout.
findings.expected: Out-of-range offsets return empty; oversized limit returns all rows.
findings.evidence: off=10001 rows=0; off=10000 rows=0; limit=0 rows=0; limit=999999 rows=10000 (5351ms).

findings.scenario: Numeric filters (between, greater_than) on a string-typed Size column at N=10000
findings.actual: between [100,200] -> 202 rows (X-Filtered=202); greater_than 5000 -> 4009 rows (X-Filtered=4009). Numeric operators correctly coerce string cell values to numbers and filter accurately. Results match the seeded distribution (Size = idx%9000+10).
findings.expected: Numeric operators coerce text values and return correct matches with accurate X-Filtered-Count.
findings.evidence: 'between [100,200]' 2288ms rows=202 X-Filt=202; 'greater_than 5000' 3352ms rows=4009 X-Filt=4009.

findings.scenario: Folder depth: nest folders toward max depth and verify the depth guard
findings.actual: Folders nest under folders successfully to depth 5; the 6th level is blocked with HTTP 400. Guard is deterministic (re-confirmed: depth-6 reliably 400). Create latency ~1.1-1.6s per level, stable. Depth-5 folder persists (re-fetched OK).
findings.expected: A depth guard returns an error past the max nesting level.
findings.evidence: depth 1-5 OK (1105-1649ms each); depth 6 BLOCKED HTTP=400; reconfirm depth-6 HTTP=400 desc='Bad Request'. Re-fetched depth-5 folder name=depth-5 parentId set.

findings.scenario: Folder depth guard error quality — does the 400 explain the limit?
findings.actual: The depth-guard 400 returns a ZERO-LENGTH body (no error message, no limit value). Same for folder-under-workbook 400. A client hitting the limit gets 'Bad Request' with no machine- or human-readable reason — no {error:...} JSON.
findings.expected: 400 should carry a clear error body (e.g. {error:'max folder depth (5) exceeded'}) so clients can surface why the request failed.
findings.evidence: depth-6 reconfirm: HTTP=400 desc='Bad Request' bodyLen=0 body=''. folder-under-workbook: code=400 msg='' (empty).
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Guard throws/returns NextResponse with status 400 but no JSON body, or the proxy strips the body on 400.

findings.scenario: Structural constraint: create a folder whose parent is a workbook
findings.actual: Creating type=folder with parentId=<workbook> is rejected with 400 (folders cannot live inside workbooks; workbooks hold tables). Top-level folder (parentId=null) and folder-under-folder both succeed. Constraint is correct — only flaw is the empty 400 body (tracked separately).
findings.expected: A folder under a workbook should be rejected.
findings.evidence: A folder-under-workbook: ok=False code=400. B top-level-folder: ok=True. C folder-under-folder: ok=True.

findings.scenario: Run a formula over the large 10,000-row sheet and time it (docs claim <2s for 10k rows)
findings.actual: POST /api/formula/run on 10,000 rows ran synchronously inline (no jobId returned), exceeded the Vercel->ACA proxy 120s limit, and failed with ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR at 120178ms. Re-fetch confirms ZERO effect: the target 'Tier' column was NEVER created and NO rows were written (big sheet still has exactly 5 columns minutes later). Work was completely lost; no async fallback, no row cap. Directly contradicts API-REFERENCE 'Formula | Instant | 10,000 rows in <2 seconds'.
findings.expected: Either complete quickly (<2s per docs) or return an async jobId to poll; the output column and values should persist.
findings.evidence: formula/run returned in 120178ms with 'ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR sin1::fhqv7-...'; post-recovery GET /api/columns shows 'Big sheet columns=5; Tier exists now=False'.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Formula endpoint evaluates all rows inline in the request lifecycle (loads whole table + computes), exceeding the edge proxy timeout; no background job, no chunking, no row limit. Same load-whole-table pattern as reads.

findings.scenario: Blast radius of the oversized 10k formula request on unrelated endpoints
findings.actual: The single 10k synchronous formula request degraded the ENTIRE backend for several minutes. During/after it, unrelated calls failed with ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR: GET /api/columns, GET /api/stats, POST /api/tables, POST /api/columns, POST /api/rows on brand-new tiny sheets. Read path recovered first (~39s to first stats OK); write path lagged badly — POST /api/columns needed 4 retries (~24s) AFTER stats was already OK, and a fresh small formula/run retried 3x. A 3-consecutive-write stability gate only passed after ~4 minutes total.
findings.expected: One heavy/oversized request must not take down the whole API; requests should be isolated (timeouts/queueing) so other tenants/sheets keep working.
findings.evidence: Repeated 'ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR' on /api/columns, /api/stats, /api/tables, /api/rows for unrelated tiny sheets; recovery poll: 'attempt 1 still down (t=30s); attempt 2 stats OK (1360ms) t=39s'; later write-pings needed full reset.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Single-container ACA backend with no per-request isolation/concurrency cap; the runaway inline formula saturates CPU/event-loop (or the container restarts), starving all other requests. Writes recover slower than reads.

findings.scenario: Formula correctness on a small sheet (isolate logic from scale)
findings.actual: Same conditional formula on a 3-row sheet produced correct values and persisted them: Size=8000->Tier=Enterprise, 2000->Mid, 100->SMB. Output column was created and re-fetch confirmed the values. Proves the 10k failure is purely scale/architecture, not a broken formula. (Reported wall time 199890ms is inflated by my retry sleeps during the backend-outage window, not actual compute.)
findings.expected: Formula evaluates ternary correctly per row and writes results to the new column.
findings.evidence: 'small (3-row) formula/run ... columnId=3wcvii7dCIVP'; re-fetch: Size=8000->Tier=Enterprise, Size=2000->Tier=Mid, Size=100->Tier=SMB.

findings.scenario: Data-movement assertion: 10,000 seeded rows persist and report correct count
findings.actual: After all seeding and the failed formula run, GET /api/rows on the big sheet reports X-Total-Count=10000 — every inserted row persisted and none were corrupted/lost by the load test or the runaway formula.
findings.expected: All 10,000 inserted rows persist and are countable.
findings.evidence: FINAL X-Total-Count on big sheet: 10000.

findings.scenario: Cascading delete of workbook (teardown) and non-empty folder delete behavior
findings.actual: DELETE on the workbook cascaded all sheets/rows/columns and returned 404 on re-fetch (clean). DELETE on a NON-EMPTY folder returned 409 Conflict (folders must be empty before deletion — matches recent 'require folders to be empty' commit), so nested folders had to be deleted deepest-first; all 5 levels then deleted and verified gone. No orphan resources left.
findings.expected: Workbook delete cascades; non-empty folder delete is guarded; deepest-first folder deletion succeeds.
findings.evidence: workbook delete -> re-fetch HTTP=404; root folder delete -> 409 Conflict; deepest-first chain: deleted ac590b63.., 1f7c0191.., f997195c.., f26db1a0.., f42587e5..; ALL_FOLDERS_GONE=True.

findings.scenario: Pipeline step 1 happy-path: import 5-row mixed-type CSV (names/emails/urls/numbers/dates) via POST /api/import/csv, then re-GET rows and columns to assert every value landed in the correctly-typed column.
findings.expected: 5 rows imported; 6 columns auto-created with inferred types text/email/url/number/date/text; each value in the right column on re-GET.
findings.actual: rowsImported=5, columnsCreated=6. Re-GET: Full Name=text, Email=email, Website=url, Employees=number, Founded=date, Domain=text. All 5 rows present, every value matched (e.g. Marc Benioff|marc@salesforce.com|https://www.salesforce.com|80000|1999-03-08|salesforce.com).
findings.evidence: X-Total-Count=5 X-Filtered-Count=5; columns: Email->email, Website->url, Employees->number, Founded->date. row0 data exactly the imported values.

findings.scenario: Cell-structure invariant on imported cells: assert {value,status,...} shape after CSV import.
findings.expected: Each cell is a well-formed object; value present and typed per column.
findings.actual: Imported cells are minimal {value} objects (no status/enrichmentData/metadata key). Value present for all cells. The doc's canonical cell shape lists status as part of the union but it is legitimately optional on plain imported cells; downstream filters and PATCH handle these cells fine.
findings.evidence: row1 every cell keys=[value]; e.g. Employees cell = {value:'80000'}.

findings.scenario: Integrity probe: number-typed column stores its value as a JSON string, not a JSON number.
findings.expected: Ideally a number column stores a numeric value; at minimum numeric operations must still work.
findings.actual: Employees (type=number) stores value as string '80000' (PowerShell reports <String> for every row). NOT a corruption: numeric filters still coerce correctly (greater_than/between work, see filter findings), so this is cosmetic at the storage layer only.
findings.evidence: All 5 Employees cells value type = String; greater_than 100000 still returned exactly the 3 expected rows.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: CSV import stores all cell values as strings regardless of inferred column type; filter engine Number()-coerces at compare time so behavior is correct.

findings.scenario: Pipeline step 2: server-side text filter contains 'salesforce' on Domain; assert X-Filtered-Count and the right subset.
findings.expected: X-Filtered-Count=1, only Marc Benioff.
findings.actual: X-Filtered-Count=1, body had 1 row = Marc Benioff.
findings.evidence: X-Filtered=1 names=Marc Benioff

findings.scenario: Numeric filter on number column stored as string: greater_than 100000 and between [30000,200000] on Employees.
findings.expected: greater_than 100000 -> Satya/Tim/Sundar (3); between 30000-200000 -> Marc(80k)/Tim(160k)/Sundar(180k) (3, Satya 220k & Jensen 29.6k excluded).
findings.actual: greater_than -> exactly Satya Nadella, Tim Cook, Sundar Pichai (3). between -> exactly Marc(80000), Tim(160000), Sundar(180000) (3). Numeric comparison works despite string storage.
findings.evidence: F2 X-Filtered=3; F3 names=Marc Benioff(80000), Tim Cook(160000), Sundar Pichai(180000)

findings.scenario: Boundary filter: is_empty on Email (no empties) and pagination edges limit=2&offset=4 / offset=10 (beyond end).
findings.expected: is_empty Email -> 0 rows; offset=4&limit=2 -> 1 row with X-Total still 5; offset=10 -> 0 rows.
findings.actual: is_empty Email X-Filtered=0. Pagination: offset0/limit2=2 rows, offset4/limit2=1 row (X-Total=5), offset10=0 rows. Correct.
findings.evidence: F4 X-Filtered=0; B3 X-Total=5 returned=1; offset10 returned=0

findings.scenario: FRAGILE regression: filter referencing a columnId that belongs to a DIFFERENT sheet (Companies Domain col used against Leads). Must return 400 invalidColumnIds, not silently match every row.
findings.expected: 400 with {error, invalidColumnIds:[...], tableId}; NOT 200 matching all rows.
findings.actual: HTTP 400 with exact documented body: {"error":"Filter references columnId(s) that do not belong to this table...","invalidColumnIds":["3bf78bfa-..."],"tableId":"3877f733-..."}. The historic silent 'match every row' bug does NOT reproduce.
findings.evidence: code=400 invalidColumnIds=[3bf78bfa-162d-422a-a1ec-2bc7aa9131f9] tableId=3877f733-7022-4451-8e9a-1d1da13fb9fc

findings.scenario: Pipeline step 3: lookup Leads.Domain against Companies.Domain, pull Industry; re-GET and assert enrichmentData carried per-row and unmatched handled.
findings.expected: 4 matched, 1 unmatched (nvidia.com absent from Companies); matched cells value='matched', status='complete', enrichmentData carries source fields with correct per-row Industry; unmatched cell value=null.
findings.actual: matched=4 unmatched=1 processed=5. Each matched cell: value='matched', status='complete', enrichmentData=[Company Name,Domain,Industry,Annual Revenue] with row-specific Industry (Salesforce=Software, Apple=Hardware, Google=Internet -> proves not a blanket match). Jensen/nvidia: value=null, enrichmentData={__no_match}.
findings.evidence: Tim Cook val=matched status=complete Industry=Hardware; Sundar Industry=Internet; Jensen val= edKeys=[__no_match]

findings.scenario: Pipeline step 4 (the single PAID call): AI enrichment on 1 row via setup-and-run, gpt-5-nano, structured outputColumns [hq_city,hq_country]; re-GET and assert enrichmentData + metadata persisted and cell shape well-formed.
findings.expected: success=1 errors=0; new enrichment column linked to config; cell {value,status,enrichmentData,rawResponse,metadata}; enrichmentData has hq_city/hq_country + auto reasoning/confidence/steps_taken; metadata has tokens/cost; NO auto-created hq_city/hq_country text columns.
findings.actual: processed=1 success=1 errors=0 totalCost=$0.0002521. Column 'HQ Location (AI)' type=enrichment enrichmentConfigId=Kxl2ID-xGCc-. Cell keys=[value,status,enrichmentData,rawResponse,metadata], status=complete, value='5 datapoints'. enrichmentData={hq_city:'San Francisco',hq_country:'United States',reasoning,confidence:'high',steps_taken}. metadata={inputTokens:157,outputTokens:591,timeTakenMs:8003,totalCost:0.0002521,webSearchCalls:0}. post-cols=8 (only +1) -> Data Guide did NOT auto-create city/country columns.
findings.evidence: enrichmentData.hq_city='San Francisco'; metadata.totalCost=0.0002521; 'city col exists? False'

findings.scenario: Formula column data movement: POST /api/formula/run to compute Clean Domain from Website; re-GET and assert computed bare domains (no {{...}} literals stored in cells).
findings.expected: Each cell holds the computed bare domain, not the {{Website}} template.
findings.actual: Formula column (type=formula) computed: salesforce.com, microsoft.com, apple.com, google.com, nvidia.com. Note: GET /api/columns shows formula='' (empty) on the column object even though it computed correctly -- the expression appears stored on a config, not echoed on the column row (minor introspection gap, no functional impact).
findings.evidence: Marc Benioff -> salesforce.com ... Jensen Huang -> nvidia.com; col type=formula formula=(empty)

findings.scenario: FRAGILE export fidelity (bug #6): POST /api/export/csv and inspect raw CSV for (a) {{...}} formula literal leak and (b) whether enrichment/hidden columns appear and with what content.
findings.expected: Export should contain the USEFUL enriched values (looked-up Industry, AI hq_city) OR at least the enrichmentData -- not internal placeholder strings. Formula columns should export computed values (they do).
findings.actual: No {{...}} leak (formula col exports computed 'salesforce.com' -- bug #6 formula-literal leak does NOT reproduce). BUT enrichment-typed columns export their internal placeholder cell.value, NOT the enriched data: lookup column 'Company Info (Lookup)' exports the literal string 'matched' (4x) and blank for the unmatched row; AI column 'HQ Location (AI)' exports '5 datapoints' (the JSON-mode summary) for the enriched row and blank for the rest. The actual enrichmentData (San Francisco, United States, Software, Hardware, Annual Revenue, etc.) appears NOWHERE in the CSV. A user exporting an enriched sheet for CRM/outreach gets useless 'matched'/'5 datapoints' cells instead of the data they paid to enrich -- arguably worse than omitting the columns because it looks like data.
findings.evidence: CSV row: 'Marc Benioff,marc@salesforce.com,https://www.salesforce.com,80000,1999-03-08,salesforce.com,matched,5 datapoints,salesforce.com'. LEAK CHECKS: contains '{{'=False, contains 'matched'=True, contains '5 datapoints'=True, contains 'San Francisco'/'hq_city'=False, contains '__no_match'=False.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Export serializer writes cell.value verbatim for every column type; for enrichment columns cell.value is an internal status/summary token ('matched','N datapoints') rather than the meaningful field(s) in cell.enrichmentData. Export should special-case enrichment columns to emit enrichmentData (or the resolved return value), or flatten enrichmentData keys into columns.

findings.scenario: Round-trip integrity: re-import the exported CSV into a fresh sheet, re-GET, assert row/column counts and key values match the original (no loss/corruption).
findings.expected: Same 9 columns, same 5 rows, plain-column values identical; types re-inferred.
findings.actual: Re-import: rows=5 cols=9 (matches export: 9 headers, 5 rows). 0 value mismatches across Full Name/Email/Employees/Domain for all 5 rows. Types re-inferred (Email->email, Website->url, Employees->number, Founded->date). Enrichment columns re-import as type=text carrying their placeholders ('matched','5 datapoints') -- consistent with the export-leak finding: the enriched MEANING is permanently destroyed by an export->reimport cycle.
findings.evidence: value mismatches=0; reimported 'Company Info (Lookup)' type=text Marc val='matched'; 'HQ Location (AI)' type=text Marc val='5 datapoints'.

findings.actual: Returned {success:true,updatedCount:1} after retrying through transient gateway 502s (first attempt hit ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR, succeeded on retry).

findings.scenario: Error lens: PATCH to /api/rows/ with an EMPTY id segment (e.g. id resolved to blank). Characterize routing.
findings.expected: An empty dynamic segment should not silently target the collection or mislead the caller.
findings.actual: PATCH /api/rows/ returns 308 Permanent Redirect -> Location:/api/rows (Next.js trailing-slash/empty-segment collapse). With a REAL id, PATCH /api/rows/{id} returns 200 and works. Risk: a client that builds the URL from a null/blank id will be redirected to the bulk route; Invoke-RestMethod/most clients do NOT replay the PATCH body across the 308, so the write silently no-ops. Informational -- not a defect of the documented happy path, but a sharp edge for clients that don't validate the id.
findings.evidence: PATCH /api/rows/ -> code=308 loc=/api/rows ; PATCH /api/rows/{valid-id} -> code=200 (Email persisted).
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Next.js route normalization redirects the empty dynamic segment to the collection path; clients should guard against empty id before calling.

findings.scenario: Error lens: GET /api/rows with missing tableId, unknown well-formed tableId, and no Authorization header.
findings.expected: missing tableId -> 400; unknown tableId -> 400/404; no auth -> 401.
findings.actual: missing tableId -> 400 {error:'tableId is required'} (good). no auth -> 401 {error:'Authentication required...'} (good). BUT unknown well-formed tableId (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) -> 200 [] (empty array) instead of 404. A typo'd/stale tableId silently returns 'no rows' rather than signalling the table does not exist, which can mask bugs in caller pipelines.
findings.evidence: E1 code=400 'tableId is required'; E2 code=200 body=[]; E3 code=401 'Authentication required...'
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Row query does not verify table existence before running the (empty) result query; add an existence check -> 404 when table missing.

findings.scenario: Error lens: POST /api/export/csv with missing tableId and with an unknown well-formed tableId.
findings.expected: missing tableId -> 400 (got it); unknown tableId -> fast 400/404.
findings.actual: missing tableId -> 400 (correct). Unknown tableId (00000000-...) -> request HANGS and times out at 25s with no response (vs GET /api/rows which returns 200 [] instantly for the same id). The export path handles a non-existent table by hanging instead of erroring -- a potential worker-tie-up / resource exhaustion vector under repeated bad input.
findings.evidence: E5 export unknown tableId: msg='The operation has timed out' after 25s (no HTTP status). E5b export missing tableId: code=400.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Export likely awaits a row stream/aggregation that never resolves (or a query that blocks) when the table doesn't exist; add an early table-existence check and a hard timeout.

findings.expected: empty data -> 400; single row -> 200 with rowsImported=1.
findings.actual: data:[] -> 400 {error:'No data provided'}. single row -> 200 {rowsImported:1, columnsCreated:2}.
findings.evidence: B1 code=400 'No data provided'; B2 code=200 rowsImported=1 columnsCreated=2

findings.scenario: Infrastructure reliability: the Vercel front-door rewrite to the Azure backend intermittently returns ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR (gateway 5xx) on otherwise-valid requests. Quantified via burst probes.
findings.expected: Valid authenticated reads should return 200 reliably.
findings.actual: Intermittent gateway failures across the whole proxied surface, NOT data corruption (every call that got through returned correct data; succeeds on retry). Burst probes: GET /api/columns = 4/8 failed, then 7/10 failed; GET /api/stats = 4/6 failed; GET /api/projects = 0/6 failed in the same window. Errors are Vercel edge errors 'ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR sin1::<id>' (Singapore edge) and one bulk-PATCH 502 -- consistent with a flapping/cold backend container behind the rewrite. For an internal rollout this produces frequent spurious failures and would require client-side retry.
findings.evidence: GET /api/columns: 4 ok / 4 failed of 8; then 3 ok / 7 failed of 10 (errCodes blank = connection/gateway-level, no HTTP body). GET /api/stats: 2 ok / 4 fail of 6. GET /api/projects: 6 ok / 0 fail of 6. Sample id: sin1::j677d-1780907910698-c4e33ff4ee40.
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Azure Container App backend behind the Vercel /api/* rewrite is scaling to zero / cold-starting or has unhealthy replicas, so bursts hit dead targets and the Vercel router returns ROUTER_EXTERNAL_TARGET_ERROR. Check ACA min-replicas/health probes and Vercel rewrite target health.

findings.scenario: Cron stepping is inconsistent: sometimes completes multiple steps in one tick, and an eager synchronous advance makes a tick report 'No active campaigns' while the step it just completed shows complete
findings.expected: Deterministic one-step-per-tick advance as documented (§16: 'advanced one step per cron tick')
findings.actual: In one run a single tick reported 'completed (all 4 steps done)' advancing TWO pending filter steps at once; in another, tick2 returned {"message":"No active campaigns"} yet the filter_rows step had just transitioned to complete. The engine appears to advance some steps eagerly/synchronously rather than strictly one per tick.
findings.evidence: TICK returned 'completed (all 4 steps done)' with steps[2] and steps[3] both flipping pending->complete; separately CRON2_RESP={"message":"No active campaigns"} while filter_rows result={"removed":1,"remaining":2}
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: Processor likely loops/chains trivial synchronous steps within a single invocation and reports state observed at scan time; messaging doesn't reflect the eager advance

findings.scenario: Concurrency: fire 3 parallel cron ticks at the same running campaign/step (no-locking probe)
findings.expected: A single step is claimed/processed by at most one concurrent invocation (mutex/row-lock); other concurrent ticks should no-op or see it already taken
findings.actual: All 3 concurrent ticks targeted the SAME step 2 (import_csv): tick1='step 2 complete... advancing to 3', tick2='step 2 still running', tick3='step 2 complete... advancing to 3'. Two invocations both claimed to COMPLETE the same step — no locking. Data happened to stay correct (rowCount=3 not 6) because import_csv is idempotent, but a non-idempotent paid step (search_companies / find_emails_waterfall) would be double-executed and double-billed.
findings.evidence: TICK1 action='step 2 complete (import_csv), advancing to 3'; TICK3 action='step 2 complete (import_csv), advancing to 3'; TICK2 action='step 2 still running (import_csv)'; post-check ROWCOUNT=3 (no duplication this time)
findings.suspectedRootCauseHint: process-campaigns selects the current step without an atomic claim (no SELECT...FOR UPDATE / status compare-and-set), so overlapping invocations (scheduled cron + manual trigger, or retry storm) can both run the same step

findings.expected: Graceful skip/error, not a crash
